pre_story_state.abandon_flow_reachability: S1 (leaveEligibility.ts), S2 (LeaveGroupButton abandon modal), and S3 (leaveGroupImpl send/purge gating) all landed prior to this story and are unit-tested in isolation, but nothing had driven them together through the real component tree — the pure functions and the modal wiring were each independently correct without proof the feature is reachable end to end (AC-STRUCT-2's stated risk).
pre_story_state.e2e_bucket: playwright.config.ts:36-41 testMatch/testIgnore split by 'groups-*.spec.ts'/'dm-*.spec.ts' glob was already in place from prior epics; no config change needed for this story.

post_story_state.app/tests/e2e/groups-abandon-last-member: new file — single-user (one browser.newContext()) test.describe.serial with 3 tests: (1) create a group and invite nobody, (2) open the leave modal on the resulting solo group and assert it renders the abandon state (abandon-group-notice + abandon-group-confirm-btn visible, last-admin-blocked-notice not visible), (3) activate abandon-group-confirm-btn, assert arrival at the group list via an anchored pathname+search match, assert the group name is gone, reload, and assert it is still gone.
post_story_state.abandon_flow_reachability: AC-FLOW-1 now drives the real LeaveGroupButton component, the real MarmotContext.getLiveMemberPubkeys / leaveGroupImpl wiring, and the real router navigation — proving S1/S2/S3 are wired together, not merely each correct in isolation.

AC-FLOW-1_url_anchor_gotcha.issue: Playwright's expect(page).toHaveURL(regex) tests the regex against the FULL absolute URL (e.g. 'http://127.0.0.1:PORT/groups/'), not the pathname. An anchored regex of the literal form /^\/groups\/?$/ (as exploration.json's trailing_slash note specifies) can therefore never match via toHaveURL — it requires the string to START with '/', but page.url() always starts with 'http://'. First run of the spec against the real dev server failed exactly this way: 'Expected pattern: /^\/groups\/?$/, Received string: "http://127.0.0.1:PORT/groups/"'.
AC-FLOW-1_url_anchor_gotcha.fix: Replaced expect(page).toHaveURL(regex) with expect.poll(() => { const u = new URL(page.url()); return u.pathname + u.search; }).toMatch(/^\/groups\/?$/) — mirrors the pathname+search extraction convention already used in groups-invite-link.spec.ts and groups-dispatch-isolation.spec.ts (getCurrentGroupId's url.match). This preserves the AC's anchoring intent (rules out '/groups/?id=...' as a false positive for 'arrived at the list') while working correctly against Playwright's actual toHaveURL/page.url() semantics.
AC-FLOW-1_url_anchor_gotcha.verified: Re-run after the fix: all 3 tests pass, twice in a row, isolated run.

playwright_browser_binary_mismatch.issue: The sandbox's ambient PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=/opt/playwright-browsers only had chromium revision 1228 installed, but app/node_modules/playwright-core (1.58.2) pins revision 1208 — browserType.launch failed with 'Executable doesn't exist at .../chromium_headless_shell-1208/...'. Also /opt/playwright-browsers was not writable by this user (EACCES on npx playwright install).
playwright_browser_binary_mismatch.fix: Not a code change — Makefile:6-19 already documents and handles exactly this shared-cache-contention class of failure by pinning PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH := $(HOME)/.cache/playwright-few and provides make ensure-playwright / make playwright. Ran `make ensure-playwright` (installed revision 1208 into the project-isolated cache) then re-ran the spec with PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=$HOME/.cache/playwright-few exported, mirroring how make test-e2e-image-sharing invokes run-e2e.mjs.
playwright_browser_binary_mismatch.note: Environment/infra friction, not a defect in this story's code or in the Makefile.
